description.en: Make an iterator that returns object over and over again. Runs indefinitely unless the times argument is specified. Roughly equivalent to: def repeat(object, times=None): # repeat(10, 3) → 10 10 10 if times is None: while True: yield object else: for i in range(times): yield object A common use for repeat is to supply a stream of constant values to map or zip: >>> list(map(pow, range(10), repeat(2))) [0, 1, 4, 9, 16, 25, 36, 49, 64, 81]

excerpt.es: Make an iterator that returns object over and over again. Runs indefinitely unless the times argument is specified. Roughly equivalent to: def repeat(object, times=None): # repeat(10, 3) → 10 10 10 if...
